No Poverty.reason: The course's analysis of property tax and institutional improvement directly relates to ensuring equal rights to economic resources (Target 1.4) and creating pro-poor policy frameworks (Target 1.b).
evidence: Theory and Practice of Property Taxation

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to ending hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to health, well-being, or related services.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops professional knowledge and analytical skills relevant for employment (Target 4.4).
evidence: 分析與解決問題的能力
培養學生對財產稅理論與實務的理解與批判能力

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to water resources, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course analyzes the economic effects of property tax and tax competition, which are fundamental to national economic growth strategies and productive activities (Target 8.3).
evidence: 財政分權與租稅競爭 I

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The topic of fiscal decentralization (財政分權) is directly linked to how local governments fund and manage public infrastructure (Target 9.1) through property tax revenues.
evidence: 財政分權與租稅競爭 I
財政分權與租稅競爭 II
財政分權與租稅競爭 III

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Analyzing property tax incidence (租稅歸宿) and discussing institutional improvements directly addresses fiscal policies for greater equality and wealth distribution (Target 10.4).
evidence: 財產稅的租稅歸宿 I

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Property tax is a primary funding source for municipal finance. Fiscal decentralization is central to sustainable urban planning and management (Target 11.3) and service provision.
evidence: 財政分權與租稅競爭 I
財政分權與租稅競爭 II

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable consumption, production patterns, or resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or resilience.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course analyzes property tax laws and discusses institutional improvements, directly contributing to developing effective, accountable, and transparent institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course on property taxation is fundamentally about domestic resource mobilization and improving national capacity for tax collection, which is the core of Target 17.1.
evidence: Theory and Practice of Property Taxation
